mnemonics: CAIN: Closure, Associative, Identity, Inverse - the four properties to check for a Group
keyPoints: A group must satisfy four axioms: closure, associativity, identity element, and inverse elements
An abelian group is a commutative group where the operation is also commutative
A subgroup is a subset of a group that itself forms a group under the same operation
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (only abelian groups are)
Forgetting to verify closure when checking if a set with an operation forms a group
Confusing the identity element (e.g., using 1 instead of 0 for additive groups)
